description_long: This is a large, weathered wooden barrel or bucket with a cylindrical shape and open top, showing significant signs of age and damage, including large holes and missing sections. The wood is stained a deep brown, with visible grain and wear, and the staves are held together by faded, possibly rusted metal hoops. On the exterior, there are red cross symbols painted on at least two sides, suggesting a prior use related to medical or emergency supplies. The ends of the barrel are roughly circular, with one end almost entirely missing, exposing the hollow interior. The surface appears rough and splintered in places, and there are no visible handles, but the structure is reinforced by the metal bands.
description: A large, damaged wooden barrel with metal hoops and red cross markings, showing heavy wear and an open, hollow interior.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from an angle showing the mostly intact end and a red cross marking on the side, with visible wood grain and metal banding.
description_view_1: This view highlights the large opening and missing sections, with a clear view of the interior and another red cross marking.
description_view_2: The open, hollow interior is most prominent here, with the damaged rim and faded red cross visible on the side.
description_view_3: The mostly intact end is shown, with the wood grain and banding visible, and a partial red cross marking on the side.
